body: Trial matches are generated by MatchMiner, an open-source precision oncology clinical trial matching platform developed at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. Patient clinical attributes — including primary diagnosis, vital status, and tumor mutational burden — along with genomic alterations are systematically queried against a curated database of clinical trials encoded in Clinical Trial Markup Language (CTML). Each trial's eligibility criteria, including required genomic alterations, cancer type specificity, and clinical parameters, are evaluated against the patient's molecular and clinical profile. Matches are ranked by specificity: exact cancer type matches are prioritized over broader pan-cancer genomic matches. Regional institutional trials are sourced directly from participating sites and reflect real-time enrollment status. ClinicalTrials.gov entries are synchronized periodically and may lag behind current enrollment or protocol amendments. Match Scores reflect the degree of alignment between patient attributes and trial eligibility criteria as assessed by the MatchMiner algorithm and should be interpreted in that context.
